strategy_update: Use a greedy graph-coloring approach that prioritizes separation of high-degree conflict vertices. First, place constraint-linked exam pairs (E10+E20, E5→E6, E3≠E4) in compatible slots. Then distribute high-conflict exams (E7, E11, E3, E4) maximally across periods (1-8). Finally, fill remaining low-conflict exams into period slots, assigning rooms based on capacity requirements and availability. Use all 8 penalty-free periods rather than compacting into 5 periods.

strategy_update: Key learnings: (1) Spread exams evenly across ALL available periods to minimize same-period conflicts. With 22 exams and 10 periods, aim for 2-3 exams per period. (2) Respect the hard constraint that exams 2 and 18 must be in the SAME period. (3) Use a round-robin assignment pattern (exam i in period (i mod 10)) to create natural separation and reduce the likelihood of conflict-prone exams clustering together. (4) Assign rooms with adequate capacity — all individual rooms can fit any exam (34-49 students), so prioritize spreading rather than room optimization.
